summary.business_type: Local Service Business (B2B professional services) — solo web design, e-commerce & SEO/GEO agency (Face Nord Graphisme, founder François-Xavier Pin) based in Puy-Sanières near Embrun, Hautes-Alpes (05), France. Monolingual French (fr-FR), Next.js 16 + Sanity CMS on Vercel.
summary.scoring_methodology: 11 specialist categories, each weighted (see 'weight' field per category, sums to 100%). health_score = sum(score * weight). The skill's default 7-category table (Technical 22%, Content 23%, On-Page 20%, Schema 10%, Performance 10%, AI Search 10%, Images 5%) was adapted to the 11 categories actually produced for this site: Sitemap/Indexation was split out of Technical, On-Page issues are folded into Technical findings (#3/#4), Images was not separately audited (no dedicated image-SEO pass run), and Visual/UX, Local SEO, SXO, Topic Clusters and Backlinks were added per the orchestrator's business-type detection (Local Service business with an active blog/content strategy).

top_findings.title: /sitemap.xml returns HTTP 404 in production
top_findings.impact: Google Search Console cannot read the sitemap at all — robots.txt points to a dead URL. Blocks discovery of new blog posts/city pages outside the main nav.

top_findings.title: 7 pages have <link rel="canonical"> pointing to the homepage instead of themselves
top_findings.impact: /portfolio, /blog, /a-propos, /contact, /faq, /mentions-legales, /politique-de-confidentialite all tell Google they are duplicates of the homepage — risk of non-indexing for 5 genuinely valuable pages.

top_findings.title: 8 of 13 blog posts (62%) contain UTF-8 replacement-character (U+FFFD) corruption; 3 are fully unreadable
top_findings.impact: Three long-form posts (324/268/136 corrupted characters each) are unusable by human readers and AI citation engines — directly undermines the GEO content strategy.

top_findings.title: Large black WebGL rendering artifacts cover the top of the mobile homepage, above the fold
top_findings.impact: The agency's own homepage looks visibly broken on mobile — the majority device for local-search traffic. Likely caused by a three.js canvas resize/devicePixelRatio bug (canvas reports 780x1688 vs CSS 390x844).

top_findings.title: Duplicate, conflicting ProfessionalService schema on the homepage with a placeholder phone number (+33600000000) shipped sitewide
top_findings.impact: Two JSON-LD blocks share the same @id with conflicting data, and the fake phone number is the only telephone Google sees for the LocalBusiness entity on 38 pages.

quick_wins.title: Strip the redundant "| Face Nord Graphisme" suffix from 6 page titles
quick_wins.benefit: Fixes 82-102 char titles that get truncated in SERPs (creation-site-internet, refonte-ai-friendly, referencement-ia, and all 3 city pages).

quick_wins.title: Demote the sitewide duplicate "Face Nord" <h1> to a non-heading element
quick_wins.benefit: One shared-component edit fixes a duplicate-H1 issue present on all 38 pages and recovers above-the-fold mobile space.

quick_wins.title: Add missing security headers (X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy) via next.config/vercel.json
quick_wins.benefit: 5 of 6 recommended headers are currently absent — a single headers() config block fixes all of them.

quick_wins.title: Move llms.txt into public/ so it stops 404ing
quick_wins.benefit: Same routing root cause as the sitemap 404; fixing the precedence issue likely resolves both files at once.

quick_wins.title: Deploy the already-written BlogPosting.inLanguage:"fr-FR" fix from locale-audit-report.md
quick_wins.benefit: Code change exists locally (uncommitted) but is not live — a deploy alone closes this gap.

categories.name: Technical SEO
what_works: robots.txt is valid: Allow:/, Disallow:/studio/, declares the (broken) sitemap
HTTPS enforced sitewide with a strong 2-year HSTS policy
All 38 crawled URLs return clean 200s — no broken links or redirect chains
Clean, descriptive, hyphenated URL structure with no query params
Fully server-rendered HTML — all content present without JS execution
/studio/ (Sanity Studio) correctly excluded from crawling and the sitemap
Per-page robots meta consistently 'index, follow' on all indexable pages

findings.title: /sitemap.xml returns HTTP 404 in production
findings.description: curl -sI returns 404 with X-Matched-Path: /[slug] — the catch-all dynamic route is intercepting the request instead of the sitemap.ts special-file route, which exists in the repo (uncommitted) but isn't being served.
findings.recommendation: Verify sitemap.ts builds and serves locally (next build / next start), resolve the routing-precedence conflict with the [slug] catch-all per this Next.js version's docs, deploy, and resubmit the sitemap in GSC.

findings.title: 7 pages have canonical pointing to the homepage instead of themselves
findings.description: /portfolio, /blog, /a-propos, /contact, /faq, /mentions-legales and /politique-de-confidentialite all emit <link rel="canonical" href="https://www.facenordgraphisme.fr">.
findings.recommendation: Fix the shared metadata helper so each route computes its own absolute canonical URL via alternates.canonical instead of hardcoding the base URL.

findings.title: Every page renders two <h1> elements; the first is an identical sitewide duplicate
findings.description:
All 38 pages emit a literal '

Face Nord

' from a shared hero/branding component, in addition to the page-specific h1.
findings.recommendation: Demote the 'Face Nord' branding element to a non-heading tag (div/span) so each page retains exactly one, page-specific h1.

findings.title: Title tag double-suffix bug on 6 pages
findings.description: Root layout's title template '%s | Face Nord Graphisme' appends a second brand suffix to 6 page-level titles that already end in '| Face Nord Graphisme', producing 82-102 char titles (e.g. /referencement-ia is 102 chars).
findings.recommendation: Remove the redundant trailing '| Face Nord Graphisme' from the 6 affected page-level titles (creation-site-internet, refonte-ai-friendly, referencement-ia, and the 3 villes pages) so the template appends it only once.

findings.title: Five of six recommended security headers are missing
findings.description: curl -sI shows only Strict-Transport-Security present. Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy and Permissions-Policy are all absent.
findings.recommendation: Add a headers() block (next.config or vercel.json) for X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN, Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin, Permissions-Policy. Treat CSP as a separate, carefully-tested follow-up.

findings.title: /llms.txt also returns 404 (same routing root cause)
findings.description: Served by the same [slug] catch-all (X-Matched-Path: /[slug]) — a static file under public/ is being shadowed by dynamic routing.
findings.recommendation: Place llms.txt under public/llms.txt and verify it serves with Content-Type: text/plain; if still shadowed, this confirms a sitewide static-file routing-precedence bug affecting both this and the sitemap.

findings.title: Orphan duplicate page /refonte-ia-friendly is live, indexable, and absent from sitemap.ts
findings.description: Returns 200, index/follow, self-canonical, linked from homepage nav, but not in sitemap.ts's static routes — its near-duplicate /refonte-ai-friendly is.
findings.recommendation: Resolve per the Content Quality duplicate-content recommendation (merge + 301), then update sitemap.ts's static routes to match the surviving URL.

categories.name: Content Quality
what_works: /a-propos carries a genuine first-person founder bio with specific local-expertise narrative — strong E-E-A-T signal
All 7 service pages are 950-1,400 words — no thin-content risk on core commercial pages
All 3 city pages are 1,200-1,400 words with city-specific FAQ blocks
11 of 13 blog posts are 2,500-3,900 words — strong topical depth for the niche
FAQPage schema present on every sampled post and all city pages
Content reads as locally-specific, no generic AI-boilerplate filler detected

findings.title: 8 of 13 blog posts (62%) contain U+FFFD encoding corruption; 3 are fully unreadable
findings.description: Byte-level scan found U+FFFD counts of 324/268/136 (full-article, unreadable), 24/20 (partial-section), and 12/8/8 (title/meta only) across 8 posts. Likely caused by double-encoded content entered into Sanity.
findings.recommendation: Inspect the raw stored Sanity field for one corrupted post to determine if the data itself is corrupted (requires retyping/restoring from revision history) vs. a rendering bug. Fix the 3 fully-corrupted posts first, then verify with the same byte-level check returning 0 sitewide.

findings.title: /refonte-ai-friendly and /refonte-ia-friendly are near-duplicate, cannibalizing pages
findings.description: Same search intent, 1,216 vs 421 words, different titles/schema; only one is in sitemap.ts.
findings.recommendation: Merge the best elements of both into one URL, 301-redirect the other, and update sitemap.ts.

findings.title: Duplicate portfolio case-study content: reves-d-aventures vs reves-daventures
findings.description: Two separate Sanity project documents (288 vs 386 words) describe the same client project under different slugs.
findings.recommendation: Consolidate into one document (keep the 386-word version, trim its 109-char title), delete the other, and 301-redirect to the hyphen-consistent slug (reves-d-aventures, matching the other 5 portfolio slugs' pattern).

findings.title: No content freshness signal: all 13 blog posts have lastUpdated: null
findings.description: sitemap.ts falls back to publishedAt || new Date() for lastModified, so the sitemap never reflects genuine content updates.
findings.recommendation: Populate lastUpdated in Sanity on every future content edit, starting with the mojibake fixes; establish a periodic freshness-review cadence per blog-strategy.md.

findings.title: Portfolio case studies are thin relative to the rest of the site
findings.description: 6 portfolio pages are 264-386 words vs. 950+ words for every other content type.
findings.recommendation: Add 2-3 paragraphs per portfolio entry covering the client's problem, solution delivered, and outcome — mirroring the blog's case-study structure.

categories.name: Sitemap & Indexation
what_works: sitemap.ts logic correctly covers 37 of 38 crawled URLs (static, city, project and post routes via Sanity)
No stale/hardcoded slugs found — city, project and post routes are all CMS-driven
Location-page quality gates (30+/50+ page thresholds) not yet triggered at 3 city pages

findings.title: /sitemap.xml returns HTTP 404 — sitemap is completely unreachable
findings.description: Same root cause as Technical Finding #1 — covered in depth there.
findings.recommendation: See Technical SEO category — fix the routing/build issue and deploy.

findings.title: robots.txt points to the broken sitemap URL
findings.description: The Sitemap: directive in robots.txt references the 404ing /sitemap.xml.
findings.recommendation: No separate action needed once the sitemap itself is fixed and deployed — robots.txt's reference is otherwise correct.

findings.title: Duplicate Sanity project documents will produce duplicate/erroneous <url> entries
findings.description: 9 project docs exist for 5 unique projects (act-event-pro, gaec-des-valentins and gaudineto each have 2 identical-slug duplicates; Rêves d'Aventures has 2 different-slug docs).
findings.recommendation: Delete the 4 redundant duplicate documents and consolidate the Rêves d'Aventures pair (see Content Quality finding) so the sitemap lists ~37 distinct URLs, not ~41.

findings.title: /refonte-ia-friendly is a live, indexable orphan page absent from sitemap.ts
findings.description: Covered in depth under Technical SEO and Content Quality.
findings.recommendation: Resolve the duplicate-page decision once, then update sitemap.ts's static routes.

findings.title: Static/city/project routes all use lastModified: new Date() — no real dates
findings.description: Every non-post route reports the build/audit date as its lastModified value, which is meaningless to crawlers.
findings.recommendation: Use real _updatedAt timestamps from Sanity for city/project routes where available; for purely static routes, omit lastModified or set it to the last actual code-deploy date.

categories.name: Schema & Structured Data
what_works: Sitewide @graph (Organization + WebSite + ProfessionalService) present on all 38 pages with correct @id anchors
inLanguage: "fr-FR" confirmed live on the WebSite node sitewide
Real ProfessionalService/LocalBusiness schema with address, geo and openingHoursSpecification
All 7 service pages carry page-specific Service schema; 3 include Offer/priceSpecification with real EUR pricing
All 3 city pages carry LocalBusiness + city-specific FAQPage blocks
All 6 portfolio pages carry CreativeWork schema cross-referencing the agency entity
All 5 sampled blog posts carry well-formed BlogPosting schema with correct ISO 8601 dates
No deprecated schema types found; all @context values are https://schema.org

findings.title: Duplicate, conflicting ProfessionalService/#localbusiness schema on the homepage with a placeholder phone number
findings.description: Homepage emits two JSON-LD blocks sharing @id '#localbusiness' with different/conflicting properties; the sitewide layout.tsx block ships telephone: "+33600000000" (placeholder) to all 38 pages.
findings.recommendation: Merge into one ProfessionalService definition in layout.tsx's @graph combining both blocks' best fields, replace the placeholder telephone with the real business number (or omit if none exists), and delete the duplicate block in page.tsx. Audit the city-pages' +33612345678 too.

findings.title: BlogPosting.inLanguage: "fr-FR" fix from locale-audit-report.md is not live
findings.description: The fix is coded in blog/[slug]/page.tsx (uncommitted) but absent from live JSON-LD on all 5 sampled posts.
findings.recommendation: Deploy the pending locale-audit-report.md changes (blog/[slug]/page.tsx, sitemap.ts, queries.ts).

findings.title: Mojibake corruption inside FAQPage JSON-LD on /blog/seo-local-hautes-alpes-artisans-pme
findings.description: The U+FFFD corruption from Content Finding #1 extends into structured-data fields, not just visible text/title tags, on this post.
findings.recommendation: Fix as part of the Content Quality mojibake remediation; re-validate this post's JSON-LD with a schema validator after the fix.

findings.title: FAQPage retained on /faq despite Google's May-2026 FAQ rich-result retirement
findings.description: No SERP rich-result benefit remains, but the markup still has GEO/AI-citation value.
findings.recommendation: No action required — informational only, keep the markup for AI/LLM citation purposes.

findings.title: No BreadcrumbList schema anywhere on the site
findings.description: Despite breadcrumb navigation being a commonly marketed deliverable, no BreadcrumbList JSON-LD was found on any sampled page.
findings.recommendation: Add BreadcrumbList schema to all non-homepage pages, reflecting the actual nav hierarchy (e.g. Home > Prestations > [Service]).

findings.title: Service schema 'provider' is a bare LocalBusiness stub, inconsistent across 6 of 7 service pages
findings.description: The provider field doesn't consistently link to the canonical #localbusiness @id.
findings.recommendation: Standardize provider to reference {"@id": "https://www.facenordgraphisme.fr/#localbusiness"} on all 7 service pages.

findings.title: City pages' LocalBusiness block duplicates and slightly conflicts with the sitewide entity
findings.description: Each city page defines its own untethered LocalBusiness block instead of referencing the sitewide #localbusiness @id.
findings.recommendation: Replace each city page's standalone LocalBusiness block with a reference to the sitewide entity plus an areaServed override, or formally model each city as a service-area Place linked to the main entity.

findings.title: Portfolio CreativeWork could be more specific
findings.description: Generic CreativeWork typing under-represents what these pages actually showcase.
findings.recommendation: Consider a more specific type (e.g. CreativeWork with 'about' pointing to the client's WebSite/Product) — low priority.

findings.title: /prestations has no Service/ItemList schema linking to the 7 individual Service entities
findings.description: The services hub page lacks an aggregating ItemList tying the 7 page-specific Service schemas together.
findings.recommendation: Add an ItemList or hasOfferCatalog on /prestations referencing the 7 Service @ids.

findings.title: No AggregateRating/Review schema anywhere
findings.description: Despite blog content extensively discussing Google reviews for clients, the agency's own site has no review/rating schema.
findings.recommendation: Once real client testimonials/reviews exist (see Local SEO finding), add Review/AggregateRating schema to the relevant entity.

categories.name: Performance (Core Web Vitals)
what_works: Desktop performance is near-perfect across home/service/blog (0.99-1.00)
CLS is excellent everywhere (0.000-0.013, all well under the 0.1 'good' threshold)
FCP is good-to-excellent even on mobile (0.9-1.2s across all 3 mobile runs)
Service page mobile (0.90) shows the 3D-hero impact is not uniformly catastrophic across page types

findings.title: Mobile homepage LCP (4.3s) falls into Google's 'Poor' band
findings.description: Home-mobile scores 0.74 overall, with LCP 4.3s (>4.0s = poor), TBT 420ms, TTI 4.8s — almost certainly driven by the 3D WebGL hero blocking the main thread on mobile.
findings.recommendation: Defer/lazy-mount the three.js hero via next/dynamic with ssr:false, mounting only after the LCP element paints (requestIdleCallback or IntersectionObserver). Confirm the actual LCP element via the lighthouse-home-mobile.json largest-contentful-paint-element audit detail.

findings.title: 98-121 KiB of unused JavaScript shipped on every page
findings.description: Consistent across all 6 Lighthouse runs (98-121 KiB), suggesting a shared bundle (three.js/@react-three/fiber/drei + GSAP + Lenis + styled-components) loaded on pages that may not need it.
findings.recommendation: Use @next/bundle-analyzer to confirm which chunks contain the 3D/animation libs and whether they're in the shared layout bundle; code-split via next/dynamic so only pages with the 3D hero pay its cost.

findings.title: Blog and service pages show the same mobile LCP/TTI pattern as home, at lower severity
findings.description: Blog-mobile LCP 4.1s/score 0.81, service-mobile LCP 3.0s/score 0.90 — same shape as home, confirming a sitewide shared-bundle root cause.
findings.recommendation: No separate fix — re-run Lighthouse on all 3 page types after the hero-deferral and bundle-splitting fixes to confirm a uniform improvement.

categories.name: AI Search Readiness (GEO)
what_works: FAQPage schema sitewide on /faq, all 3 city pages, and all sampled blog posts supports AI Overviews/Perplexity citation patterns
ProfessionalService/LocalBusiness schema with GeoCoordinates/PostalAddress grounds the entity for local AI queries

Best-performing posts (4-signaux-citation-ia-artisans, geo-referencement-ia-artisans-pme-hautes-alpes) are close to fully compliant with the blog-strategy.md GEO template, demonstrating the template works when followed
PerplexityBot and GPTBot are unblocked in robots.txt

findings.title: /llms.txt does not exist (404 via catch-all route)
findings.description: Shares the same routing root cause as the sitemap 404 (Technical Finding #1/#6).
findings.recommendation: Add public/llms.txt with a structured summary of site content/sections once the routing fix lands.

findings.title: No RSL 1.0 licensing signal present
findings.description: No machine-readable content-licensing signal for AI training/crawling consent.
findings.recommendation: Consider adding an RSL declaration if the agency wants explicit control over AI training use of its content — optional, lower priority for a local-service site.

findings.title: Explicit per-bot AI crawler rules in robots.txt would add clarity
findings.description: Current robots.txt uses a single User-Agent: * rule; nothing is currently blocked, but explicit GPTBot/PerplexityBot/ClaudeBot entries would document intent.
findings.recommendation: Optional — add explicit Allow rules per major AI crawler for documentation/clarity, no functional change expected.

findings.title: Named statistical sources are not hyperlinked as external citations
findings.description: Blog posts cite statistics by name/source but don't link out to the original source — a documented best practice for AI-citation credibility.
findings.recommendation: Add outbound links to original sources for cited statistics across the blog corpus, prioritizing the highest-traffic pillar posts first.

findings.title: prix-site-internet-artisan-pme-hautes-alpes falls short of the GEO standard on every axis
findings.description: This post (also affected by mojibake, Content Finding #1) underperforms the blog-strategy.md GEO checklist across formatting, citation, and question-format criteria.
findings.recommendation: After fixing its encoding corruption, rewrite this post against the full blog-strategy.md GEO checklist as a priority — it's currently the weakest post on two dimensions simultaneously.

findings.title: H2 question-format ratio is below the 60-70% target on 4 of 5 sampled articles
findings.description: Most sampled posts don't hit the documented question-style-heading ratio that correlates with AI Overview extraction.
findings.recommendation: When revising posts, convert more H2s to natural-language question format (e.g. "Combien coûte un site internet pour artisan ?") per the blog-strategy.md template.

findings.title: Off-site brand-mention signals are largely unbuilt
findings.description: The channels correlating most strongly with AI citation (third-party brand mentions, directory listings, press) are minimal — consistent with the Backlinks category finding that the domain isn't yet in Common Crawl.
findings.recommendation: Cross-ref Backlink Profile findings — building local citations and directory presence serves this GEO goal too.

categories.name: Visual & Mobile UX
what_works: No horizontal overflow on any of the 8 desktop/mobile captures
Cookie-consent banner renders correctly and accessibly on mobile with clear Refuser/Accepter buttons
Desktop layouts are clean across all 4 sampled page types with no rendering artifacts
Typography hierarchy is legible and consistent across page types and devices
No JavaScript runtime errors (page_errors: []) on any capture

findings.title: Large black rendering artifacts cover the top-right of the mobile homepage, above the fold
findings.description: home-mobile-fold-top-crop.png shows solid-black rectangles overlapping the hero headline. capture-results.json shows the canvas backing buffer is 780x1688 vs the 390x844 CSS viewport (exactly 2x DPR), consistent with a three.js resize/devicePixelRatio handling bug that leaves part of the backing buffer uncleared/unrendered.
findings.recommendation: Reproduce in Chrome DevTools mobile emulation at DPR 2 vs DPR 1 to confirm the hypothesis. In the @react-three/fiber setup, ensure renderer.setPixelRatio() is called before renderer.setSize(width, height, false), and camera.updateProjectionMatrix() runs after any resize. Treat as a P0 bug independent of SEO — likely affects real visitor conversion.

findings.title: Duplicate 'Face Nord' H1 is visually confirmed on every sampled page/device
findings.description: Confirms Technical Finding #3 visually; on mobile this element sits between the page H1 and the fold, competing for limited above-the-fold space.
findings.recommendation: Same fix as Technical Finding #3 — demoting it also recovers above-the-fold mobile space.

findings.title: Console warnings on every page: deprecated THREE.Clock and GPU stalls on desktop home
findings.description: 'THREE.THREE.Clock: deprecated' on all 8 captures; home-desktop additionally logs 4x 'GPU stall due to ReadPixels' warnings indicating a synchronous gl.readPixels call blocking the render pipeline.
findings.recommendation: Migrate to THREE.Timer per the deprecation notice; locate and remove/gate the gl.readPixels call (likely a leftover debug/screenshot utility) to reduce main-thread blocking.

categories.name: Local SEO
what_works: A real ProfessionalService/LocalBusiness schema exists with PostalAddress and GeoCoordinates
All 3 city pages have city-specific FAQ content tailored to local audiences (tourism, seasonal businesses)
/a-propos provides a strong, locally-grounded founder narrative reinforcing the local-expertise positioning
Service area explicitly named (Embrun, Gap, Briançon, Guillestre) in page content and schema

findings.title: Schema telephone is a fake placeholder number, conflicting with the real visible number, and differs between schema blocks
findings.description: Sitewide schema ships +33600000000 (placeholder pattern) while city pages ship a different +33612345678 — neither may be the real business line. Cross-ref Schema Finding #1.
findings.recommendation: Determine the real business phone number (if any) and use it consistently across all schema blocks and visible contact info; if no public phone line exists, omit telephone entirely rather than shipping a placeholder.

findings.title: Three conflicting/duplicated LocalBusiness-type schema blocks across the site
findings.description: Homepage duplicate (Schema Finding #1) plus untethered per-city LocalBusiness blocks (Schema Finding #7) create multiple, inconsistent representations of the same entity.
findings.recommendation: Consolidate to one canonical #localbusiness entity referenced everywhere via @id, per the Schema category recommendations.

findings.title: No AggregateRating/Review schema and zero visible review/testimonial content for the agency itself
findings.description: Despite the blog extensively covering how clients should manage Google reviews, the agency's own site shows no client testimonials or review signals.
findings.recommendation: Collect and display 3-5 real client testimonials (the 5 confirmed portfolio clients are a natural source — cross-ref Backlinks) with Review/AggregateRating schema once available.

findings.title: No Google Business Profile link, Maps embed, or GBP-derived content anywhere on the site
findings.description: For a local-service business, a GBP link/embed is a standard trust and local-pack signal that's currently absent.
findings.recommendation: Add a GBP profile link (and/or embedded Map) to the footer or /contact page, and ensure NAP details match the GBP listing exactly.

findings.title: Footer social icons are dead placeholder links (href="#")
findings.description: Social icons in the footer don't point anywhere — a minor trust/completeness signal and a missed off-site profile-linking opportunity.
findings.recommendation: Link to real, active social profiles (or remove the icons if none exist) — also helps the off-site brand-mention gap noted in GEO findings.

findings.title: addressLocality spelling inconsistency: 'Puy Sanières' vs 'Puy-Sanières'
findings.description: Different schema blocks/pages spell the town name with and without the hyphen — a NAP-consistency issue.
findings.recommendation: Standardize on the official spelling 'Puy-Sanières' everywhere (schema, visible address, GBP).

findings.title: Homepage hero/meta framing says 'Basé à Embrun' while the legal address is Puy-Sanières
findings.description: Marketing copy and the legal/schema address name different towns (Puy-Sanières is a small village near Embrun) — potentially confusing for NAP matching.
findings.recommendation: Either consistently use 'Puy-Sanières (près d'Embrun)' or clarify the relationship in both the visible copy and schema so automated NAP-matching tools and readers see a consistent location story.

findings.title: City-page LocalBusiness schema lacks geo and openingHoursSpecification
findings.description: Unlike the sitewide entity, the 3 city pages' LocalBusiness blocks omit geo coordinates and hours.
findings.recommendation: Resolved naturally if city pages reference the sitewide #localbusiness entity instead of duplicating it (Schema Finding #7).

findings.title: No structured citation footprint beyond LinkedIn detectable
findings.description: No Pages Jaunes, CCI, or tourism-directory listings found — consistent with the Backlinks category's 'not yet in Common Crawl' finding.
findings.recommendation: Cross-ref Backlinks Finding #1 — build a baseline local citation profile.

findings.title: No 'service area' Service/OfferCatalog markup tying services to specific cities
findings.description: The 7 Service schemas and 3 city pages aren't cross-linked via structured data (e.g. a Service offered in a given areaServed).
findings.recommendation: Lower priority — consider an OfferCatalog or areaServed cross-reference once the higher-severity schema consolidation (Finding #2) is done.

categories.name: Search Experience (SXO)
what_works: Most landing pages match their target query's dominant intent (commercial/informational split is broadly correct)
City pages correctly target local-modifier queries with dedicated FAQ content

findings.title: /refonte-ai-friendly vs /refonte-ia-friendly — duplicate-intent pages competing with each other
findings.description: Same underlying issue as Content Finding #2 and Technical Finding #7, viewed from a search-experience lens: a searcher (and Google) cannot tell which page is the 'real' answer to this query.
findings.recommendation: Resolve once (merge + redirect) — fixes this issue across Technical, Content, and SXO simultaneously.

findings.title: /referencement-ia (service page) is now out-ranked in depth by the site's own blog pillar for the same query cluster
findings.description: The blog post geo-referencement-ia-artisans-pme-hautes-alpes is more comprehensive than the commercial service page targeting overlapping GEO/AI-SEO queries — internal cannibalization between content types.
findings.recommendation: Differentiate intent clearly: keep /referencement-ia focused on the commercial offer (pricing, process, CTA) and use the blog pillar for the comprehensive educational treatment, with strong internal links from the pillar to the service page.

findings.title: City pages duplicate service-page sections rather than purely localizing intent
findings.description: Some city-page content repeats service-page copy with the city name swapped in, rather than addressing genuinely city-specific search intent (local landmarks, local client examples, local search behavior).
findings.recommendation: As city pages are revised, replace templated service-copy repetition with city-specific proof points (named local clients/projects already in the portfolio, local events, local search trends).

findings.title: Persona-page fit scoring across 4 personas x top landing pages
findings.description: Detailed persona-fit matrix available in findings/sxo.md for the 4 personas defined in blog-strategy.md.
findings.recommendation: Use the persona-fit matrix in findings/sxo.md when planning Phase 3 content work to ensure each persona has at least one strongly-matched landing page.

categories.name: Topic Clusters & Content Architecture
what_works: blog-strategy.md's 5-cluster plan is partially implemented with 9-13 posts published
Several posts demonstrate the intended hub-and-spoke linking pattern when followed correctly

findings.title: Two internal links are broken (404)
findings.description: Quick-win fix that resolves inbound-link orphaning for at least one post.
findings.recommendation: Identify and fix the 2 broken internal links (see findings/cluster.md for the specific source/target pairs).

findings.title: reservation-directe-eviter-commissions-booking-hautes-alpes is a complete orphan (zero inbound internal links)
findings.description: No other page on the site links to this post, reducing its discoverability and topical authority contribution.
findings.recommendation: Add contextual internal links from related posts (e.g. the tourism/reservation cluster) and from the relevant service page.

findings.title: Cluster 4 (E-commerce) has zero posts
findings.description: Confirmed empty as flagged in blog-strategy.md's original gap analysis.
findings.recommendation: Per blog-strategy.md's roadmap, prioritize 1-2 e-commerce-cluster posts to support /boutique-e-commerce-hautes-alpes.

findings.title: Cluster 2 (Création de Site) and Cluster 5 (Tourisme) have no internal hub-spoke structure
findings.description: Posts in these clusters are loosely cross-linked but not organized around a pillar page.
findings.recommendation: Designate a pillar post per cluster and add 'related articles' style internal links from spoke posts back to the pillar.

findings.title: Cross-cluster and cross-cluster-to-service-page linking is almost entirely nav-menu boilerplate
findings.description: Contextual, in-content editorial links between related blog posts and the service pages they support are largely absent.
findings.recommendation: When revising posts (e.g. during the mojibake fix pass), add 2-3 contextual in-body links to related posts and the relevant service/city pages.

findings.title: Cannibalization risk: /referencement-ia vs /blog/geo-referencement-ia-artisans-pme-hautes-alpes
findings.description: Same issue as SXO Finding #2, viewed from a content-architecture lens.
findings.recommendation: See SXO Finding #2.

findings.title: Blog index has no cluster/category navigation
findings.description: Clusters defined in blog-strategy.md are invisible to users and crawlers on /blog — no category filter or grouping.
findings.recommendation: Add category/cluster taxonomy to the Sanity post schema and surface it as filterable groups on /blog, reinforcing topical clustering signals.

categories.name: Backlink Profile
what_works: 5 confirmed, live, dofollow backlinks from real client websites with brand-consistent anchor text
All 5 referring domains are topically and geographically relevant (Hautes-Alpes local businesses)
None of the confirmed links carry nofollow/sponsored — all pass link equity

findings.title: Domain not yet indexed in Common Crawl; no broader off-site link profile detected
findings.description: Common Crawl (cc-main-2026-jan-feb-mar) returns in_crawl: false with no referring domains beyond the 5 confirmed via direct verification — consistent with a genuinely new/small domain.
findings.recommendation: Build a baseline local citation profile (Pages Jaunes, GBP, CCI Hautes-Alpes, tourism-office directories) — re-check Common Crawl in 2-3 months as new releases publish.

findings.title: One of five client credit-links originates from a non-HTTPS site (gaudineto.fr)
findings.description: The agency's own portfolio showcases a client deliverable still running without TLS.
findings.recommendation: Migrate gaudineto.fr to HTTPS (free via Let's Encrypt on most hosts) — improves both the client site and this referring link's value.

findings.title: Anchor-text diversity is minimal (4 of 5 links use near-identical brand anchor text)
findings.description: Not a penalty risk, but means zero current anchor-text signal for target service/location keywords.
findings.recommendation: As new client sites launch, naturally vary credit-link anchor text toward service/location phrases alongside the brand name.

phases.name: Phase 1: Critical Fixes
items: Fix /sitemap.xml 404 — resolve routing precedence vs. the [slug] catch-all, deploy, resubmit in GSC (Technical #1 / Sitemap #1)
Fix the 7 self-canonical violations pointing to the homepage (Technical #2)
Resolve the duplicate/conflicting LocalBusiness schema and remove the placeholder telephone +33600000000 sitewide (Schema #1 / Local #1 / Local #2)
Diagnose and fix U+FFFD mojibake corruption in 8 blog posts, prioritizing the 3 fully-corrupted ones (Content #1)
Fix the black WebGL canvas artifact on mobile homepage above the fold (Visual #1)
Decide and merge /refonte-ai-friendly vs /refonte-ia-friendly into one URL with a 301 redirect (Content #2 / SXO #1 / Technical #7)

phases.name: Phase 2: High-Impact Improvements
items: Demote the sitewide duplicate 'Face Nord' <h1> to a non-heading element (Technical #3 / Visual #2)
Strip the redundant '| Face Nord Graphisme' suffix from the 6 affected page titles (Technical #4)
Add missing security headers via next.config/vercel.json (Technical #5)
Move llms.txt to public/ and verify it serves correctly (Technical #6 / GEO #1)
Deploy the pending BlogPosting.inLanguage:"fr-FR" fix from locale-audit-report.md (Schema #2)
Delete the 4 redundant duplicate Sanity project documents and consolidate reves-d-aventures / reves-daventures with a 301 (Content #3 / Sitemap #3)
Fix the 2 broken internal links identified in the cluster analysis (Cluster #1)
Add BreadcrumbList schema to all non-homepage pages (Schema #5)
Defer/lazy-mount the 3D hero so it loads after LCP on mobile (Performance #1)

phases.name: Phase 3: Content & Authority
items: Standardize 'Puy-Sanières' spelling and reconcile the 'Basé à Embrun' framing with the legal address everywhere (Local #6 / Local #7)
Add a Google Business Profile link/Maps embed and ensure NAP consistency (Local #4)
Replace dead footer social links with real profiles (Local #5)
Collect 3-5 real client testimonials and add Review/AggregateRating schema (Local #3 / Schema #10)
Build hub-spoke internal linking for Cluster 2 (Création de Site) and Cluster 5 (Tourisme) (Cluster #4)
Differentiate /referencement-ia (commercial) from its blog pillar (educational) and cross-link them (Cluster #6 / SXO #2)
Hyperlink named statistics to their original sources across the blog corpus, starting with pillar posts (GEO #4)
Increase the H2 question-format ratio toward the 60-70% target on revised posts (GEO #6)
Begin populating lastUpdated in Sanity on every content edit (Content #4)
Build a baseline local citation profile: Pages Jaunes, CCI Hautes-Alpes, tourism-office directories (Backlinks #1)
Code-split three.js/GSAP/Lenis out of the shared bundle (Performance #2)

phases.name: Phase 4: Monitoring & Iteration
items: Re-run Lighthouse on home/blog/service mobile after the hero-deferral and bundle-splitting fixes to confirm uniform improvement (Performance #3)
Migrate gaudineto.fr to HTTPS (Backlinks #2)
Vary credit-link anchor text on new portfolio projects (Backlinks #3)
Add cluster/category navigation to the blog index (Cluster #7)
Add Service/OfferCatalog area-served markup once schema consolidation is complete (Local #10)
Expand portfolio case studies with process/outcome detail (Content #5)
Re-run the Common Crawl backlink check in 2-3 months (Backlinks #1 follow-up)
Establish a quarterly content-freshness review per blog-strategy.md's roadmap, prioritizing prix-site-internet-artisan-pme-hautes-alpes (GEO #5)
